 {"id":"bd-2vh3","title":"Ephemeral issue cleanup and history compaction","description":"## Problem

Beads history grows without bound. Every message, handoff, work assignment
stays in issues.jsonl forever. Enterprise users will balk at "git as database."

## Solution: Two-Tier Cleanup

### Tier 1: Ephemeral Cleanup (v1)

bd cleanup --ephemeral --closed

- Deletes closed issues where ephemeral=true from issues.jsonl
- Safe: only removes explicitly marked ephemeral + closed
- Preserves git history (commits still exist)
- Run after swarm completion

### Tier 2: History Compaction (v2)

bd compact --squash

- Rewrites issues.jsonl to remove tombstones
- Optionally squashes git history (interactive rebase equivalent)
- Preserves Merkle proofs for deleted items
- Advanced: cold storage tiering

## HOP Context

| Layer | HOP Role | Persistence |
|-------|----------|-------------|
| Execution trace | None | Ephemeral |
| Work scaffolding | None | Summarizable |
| Work outcome | CV entry | Permanent |
| Validation record | Stake proof | Permanent |

"Execution is ephemeral. Outcomes are permanent. You can't squash your CV."

## Success Criteria

- After cleanup --ephemeral: issues.jsonl only contains persistent work
- Work outcomes preserved (CV entries)
- Validation records preserved (stake proofs)
- Execution scaffolding removed (transient coordination)","notes":"## Implementation Plan (REVISED after code review)

See history/EPHEMERAL_MOLECULES_DESIGN.md for comprehensive design + review.

## Key Simplification

After code review, Tier 1 is MUCH simpler than originally designed:

- **Original**: Separate ephemeral repo with routing.ephemeral config
- **Revised**: Just set Ephemeral: true in cloneSubgraph()

The ephemeral field and bd cleanup --ephemeral already exist\!

## Child Tasks (in dependency order)

1. **bd-2vh3.2**: Tier 1 - Ephemeral spawning (SIMPLIFIED) [READY]
   - Just add Ephemeral: true to template.go:474
   - Add --persistent flag to opt out
2. **bd-2vh3.3**: Tier 2 - Basic bd mol squash command
3. **bd-2vh3.4**: Tier 3 - AI-powered squash summarization
4. **bd-2vh3.5**: Tier 4 - Auto-squash on molecule completion
5. **bd-2vh3.6**: Tier 5 - JSONL archive rotation (DEFERRED: post-1.0)

## What Already Exists

| Component | Location |
|-----------|----------|
| Ephemeral field | internal/types/types.go:45 |
| bd cleanup --ephemeral | cmd/bd/cleanup.go:72 |
| cloneSubgraph() | cmd/bd/template.go:456 |
| loadTemplateSubgraph() | cmd/bd/template.go |

## HOP Alignment

'Execution is ephemeral. Outcomes are permanent. You can't squash your CV.'","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-20T21:02:20.101367-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T13:18:44.500038-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-2vh3.1","title":"Tier 1: Ephemeral repo routing","description":"Add routing.ephemeral config option to route ephemeral=true issues to separate location.

## Changes Required

1. Add `routing.ephemeral` config option (default: empty = disabled)
2. Update routing logic in `determineRepo()` to check ephemeral flag
3. Update `bd create` to respect ephemeral routing
4. Update import/export for multi-location support
5. Ephemeral repo can be:
   - Separate git repo (~/.beads-ephemeral)
   - Non-git directory (just filesystem)
   - Same repo, different branch (future)

## Config

```bash
bd config set routing.ephemeral "~/.beads-ephemeral"
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- `bd create "test" --ephemeral` creates in ephemeral repo when configured
- `bd list` shows issues from both repos
- Ephemeral repo never synced to remote","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:57:26.648052-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T12:59:01.815357-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-21T12:59:01.815357-08:00","deleted_by":"stevey","delete_reason":"manual delete","original_type":"task"}
 {"id":"bd-2vh3.2","title":"Tier 1: Ephemeral repo routing","description":"Simplified: Make mol spawn set ephemeral=true on spawned issues.

## The Fix

Modify cloneSubgraph() in template.go to set Ephemeral: true:

```go
// template.go:474
newIssue := &types.Issue{
    Title:      substituteVariables(oldIssue.Title, vars),
    // ... existing fields ...
    Ephemeral:  true,  // ADD THIS LINE
}
```

## Optional: Add --persistent flag

Add flag to bd mol spawn for when you want spawned issues to persist:

```bash
bd mol spawn mol-code-review --var pr=123  # ephemeral (default)
bd mol spawn mol-code-review --var pr=123 --persistent  # not ephemeral
```

## Why This Is Simpler Than Original Design

Original design proposed separate ephemeral repo routing. After code review:

- Ephemeral field already exists in schema
- bd cleanup --ephemeral already works
- No new config needed
- No multi-repo complexity

## Acceptance Criteria

- bd mol spawn creates issues with ephemeral=true
- bd cleanup --ephemeral -f deletes them after closing
- --persistent flag opts out of ephemeral
- Existing molecules continue to work","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:57:36.661604-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T13:43:22.990244-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T13:43:22.990244-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented: mol spawn now creates ephemeral issues by default. Use --persistent to opt out. Changes in template.go, mol.go, mol_spawn.go, mol_bond.go, mol_run.go.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vh3.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2vh3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:57:36.662118-08:00","created_by":"stevey"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2vh3.3","title":"Tier 2: Basic bd mol squash command","description":"Add bd mol squash command for basic molecule execution compression.

## Command

bd mol squash <molecule-id> [flags]
  --dry-run         Preview what would be squashed
  --keep-children   Don't delete ephemeral children after squash
  --json            JSON output

## Implementation

1. Find all ephemeral children of molecule (parent-child deps)
2. Concatenate child descriptions/notes into digest
3. Create digest issue in main repo with:
   - Title: 'Molecule Execution Summary: <original-title>'
   - digest_of: [list of squashed child IDs]
   - ephemeral: false (digest is permanent)
4. Delete ephemeral children (unless --keep-children)
5. Link digest to parent work item

## Schema Changes

Add to Issue struct:
- SquashedAt *time.Time
- SquashDigest string (ID of digest)
- DigestOf []string (IDs of squashed children)

## Acceptance Criteria

- bd mol squash <id> creates digest, removes children
- --dry-run shows preview
- Digest has proper metadata linking","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:57:48.338114-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T12:57:48.338114-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vh3.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2vh3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:57:48.338636-08:00","created_by":"stevey"},{"issue_id":"bd-2vh3.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2vh3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:22.601321-08:00","created_by":"stevey"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2vh3.4","title":"Tier 3: AI-powered squash summarization","description":"Use Haiku for intelligent molecule execution summarization.

## Enhancement to bd mol squash

Instead of simple concatenation, use Claude Haiku to generate intelligent summary:

## Summary Format

- Execution metrics (duration, steps completed)
- Key decisions made during execution
- Discoveries (new issues filed)
- Changes (files modified, tests added)
- Validation status

## Config

bd config set squash.model 'claude-3-5-haiku-20241022'
bd config set squash.max_tokens 2000

## Implementation

1. Collect all child issue content
2. Build prompt for Haiku summarization
3. Generate structured digest
4. Handle API errors gracefully (fallback to concatenation)

## Acceptance Criteria

- Digest is coherent summary, not just concatenation
- Key decisions extracted from verbose logs
- Metrics computed (duration, changes, etc.)
- Works without API key (fallback mode)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:00.732749-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:00.732749-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vh3.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2vh3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:00.733264-08:00","created_by":"stevey"},{"issue_id":"bd-2vh3.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2vh3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:22.698686-08:00","created_by":"stevey"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vh3.5","title":"Tier 4: Auto-squash on molecule completion","description":"Automatically squash molecules when they reach terminal state.

## Integration Points

1. Hook into molecule completion handler
2. Detect when all steps are done/failed
3. Trigger squash automatically

## Config

bd config set mol.auto_squash true  # Default: false
bd config set mol.auto_squash_on_success true  # Only on success
bd config set mol.auto_squash_delay '5m'  # Wait before squash

## Implementation Options

### Option A: Post-Completion Hook
In mol completion handler:
- Check if auto_squash enabled
- Call Squash() after terminal state

### Option B: Git Hook
In .beads/hooks/post-commit:
- bd mol squash --auto

### Option C: Daemon Background Task
- Daemon periodically checks for squashable molecules
- Squashes in background

## Acceptance Criteria

- Completed molecules auto-squash without manual intervention
- Configurable delay before squash
- Option to squash only on success vs always
- Works with both daemon and no-daemon modes","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:13.345577-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:13.345577-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vh3.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vh3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:13.346152-08:00","created_by":"stevey"},{"issue_id":"bd-2vh3.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vh3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:22.797141-08:00","created_by":"stevey"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vh3.6","title":"Tier 5 (Future): JSONL archive rotation","description":"Periodic rotation of issues.jsonl for long-running repos.

## Design

.beads/
├── issues.jsonl           # Current (hot)
├── archive/
│   ├── issues-2025-12.jsonl.gz  # Archived (cold)
│   └── ...
└── index.jsonl            # Merged index for queries

## Commands

bd archive rotate [flags]
  --older-than N    Archive issues closed > N days
  --compress        Gzip archives
  --dry-run         Preview

bd archive list           # Show archived periods
bd archive restore <period>  # Restore from archive

## Config

bd config set archive.enabled true
bd config set archive.rotate_days 90
bd config set archive.compress true
bd config set archive.path '.beads/archive'

## Considerations

- Archives can be gitignored (local only) or committed (shared)
- Query layer must check index, hydrate from archive
- Cold storage tiering (S3/GCS) for enterprise
- Merkle proofs preserved for audit

## Priority

This is post-1.0 work. Current focus is on squash (removes ephemeral).
Archive helps with long-term history but is less critical.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:38.210008-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:38.210008-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vh3.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2vh3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:38.210543-08:00","created_by":"stevey"}]}

 {"id":"bd-dsdh","title":"Document sync.branch 'always dirty' working tree behavior","description":"## Context

When sync.branch is configured, the .beads/issues.jsonl file in main's working tree is ALWAYS dirty. This is by design:

1. bd sync commits to beads-sync branch (via worktree)
2. bd sync copies JSONL to main's working tree (so CLI commands work)
3. This copy is NOT committed to main (to reduce commit noise)

Contributors who watch main branch history pushed for sync.branch to avoid constant beads commit noise. But users need to understand the trade-off.

## Documentation Needed

Update README.md sync.branch section with:

1. **Clear explanation** of why .beads/ is always dirty on main
2. **"Be Zen about it"** - this is expected, not a bug
3. **Workflow options:**
   - Accept dirty state, use `bd sync --merge` periodically to snapshot to main
   - Or disable sync.branch if clean working tree is more important
4. **Shell alias tip** to hide beads from git status:
   ```bash
   alias gs='git status -- ":!.beads/"'
   ```
5. **When to merge**: releases, milestones, or periodic snapshots

## Related

- bd-7b7h: Fix that allows bd sync --merge to work with dirty .beads/

 {"id":"bd-elqd","title":"Systematic bd sync stability investigation","description":"## Context

bd sync has chronic instability issues that have persisted since inception:
- issues.jsonl is always dirty after push
- bd sync often creates messes requiring manual cleanup
- Problems escalating despite accumulated bug fixes
- Workarounds are getting increasingly draconian

## Goal

Systematically observe and diagnose bd sync failures rather than applying band-aid fixes.

## Approach

1. Start fresh session with latest binary (all fixes applied)
2. Run bd sync and carefully observe what happens
3. Document exact sequence of events when things go wrong
4. File specific issues for each discrete problem identified
5. Track the root causes, not just symptoms

## Test Environment

- Fresh clone or clean state
- Latest bd binary with all bug fixes
- Monitor both local and remote JSONL state
- Check for timing issues, race conditions, merge conflicts

## Success Criteria

- Identify root causes of sync instability
- Create actionable issues for each problem
- Eventually achieve stable bd sync (no manual intervention needed)","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T22:57:25.35289-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T16:11:17.070763-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-17T16:11:17.070763-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"task"}
 {"id":"bd-etyv","title":"Smart --var detection for mol distill","description":"Implemented bidirectional syntax support for mol distill --var flag.

**Problem:**
- spawn uses: --var variable=value (assignment style)
- distill used: --var value=variable (substitution style)
- Agents would naturally guess spawn-style for both

**Solution:**
Smart detection that accepts BOTH syntaxes by checking which side appears in the epic text:
- --var branch=feature-auth → finds 'feature-auth' in text → works
- --var feature-auth=branch → finds 'feature-auth' in text → also works

**Changes:**
- Added parseDistillVar() with smart detection
- Added collectSubgraphText() helper
- Restructured runMolDistill to load subgraph before parsing vars
- Updated help text to document both syntaxes
- Added comprehensive tests in mol_test.go

**Edge cases handled:**
- Both sides found: prefers spawn-style (more common guess)
- Neither found: helpful error message
- Empty sides: validation error
- Values containing '=' (e.g., KEY=VALUE): works via SplitN

Embodies the Beads philosophy: watch what agents do, make their guess correct.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T11:08:50.83923-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T11:08:56.432536-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T11:08:56.432536-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented"}

 {"id":"bd-eyto","title":"Time-dependent tests may be flaky near TTL boundary","description":"Several tombstone merge tests use time.Now() to create test data: time.Now().Add(-24 * time.Hour), time.Now().Add(-60 * 24 * time.Hour), etc. While these work reliably in practice (24h vs 30d TTL has large margin), they could theoretically be flaky if: 1) Tests run slowly, 2) System clock changes during test, 3) TTL constants change. Recommendation: Consider using a fixed reference time or time injection for deterministic tests. Lower priority since current margin is large. Files: internal/merge/merge_test.go:1337-1338, 1352-1353, 1548-1549, 1590-1591","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-05T16:37:02.348143-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-05T16:37:02.348143-08:00"}

**Molecule**: Version Bump: test-squash
**Steps**: 8

**Completed**: 0/8

---

### Steps

1. **[open]** Verify release artifacts
   Check GitHub releases page - binaries for darwin/linux/windows should be available

2. **[open]** Commit and push release
   git add -A && git commit && git push to trigger CI

3. **[open]** Update CHANGELOG.md with release notes
   Add meaningful release notes to CHANGELOG.md describing what changed in test-squash

4. **[open]** Wait for CI to pass
   Monitor GitHub Actions - all checks must pass before release artifacts are built

5. **[open]** Restart running daemons
   Kill and restart any running bd daemons to pick up new version: pkill -f 'bd daemon' && bd daemon --start

6. **[open]** Update local installation
   Run install script or brew upgrade to get new version locally: curl -fsSL .../install.sh | bash

7. **[open]** Run bump-version.sh test-squash
   Run ./scripts/bump-version.sh test-squash to update version in all files

8. **[open]** Update info.go versionChanges
   Add entry to versionChanges in cmd/bd/info.go with agent-actionable changes for test-squash

 {"id":"bd-gxq","title":"Simplify bd onboard to minimal AGENTS.md snippet pointing to bd prime","description":"## Context
GH#604 raised concerns about bd onboard bloating AGENTS.md with ~100+ lines of static instructions that:
- Load every session whether beads is being used or not
- Get stale when bd upgrades
- Waste tokens

## Solution
Simplify `bd onboard` to output a minimal snippet (~2 lines) that points to `bd prime`:

```markdown
## Issue Tracking
This project uses beads (`bd`) for issue tracking.
Run `bd prime` for workflow context, or hooks auto-inject it.
```

## Rationale
- `bd prime` is dynamic, concise (~80 lines), and always matches installed bd version
- Hooks already auto-inject `bd prime` at session start when .beads/ detected
- AGENTS.md only needs to mention beads exists, not contain full instructions

## Implementation
1. Update `cmd/bd/onboard.go` to output minimal snippet
2. Keep `--output` flag for BD_GUIDE.md generation (may still be useful)
3. Update help text to explain the new approach","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-18T11:42:38.604891-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T11:47:28.020419-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-18T11:47:28.020419-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented: bd onboard now outputs minimal snippet pointing to bd prime"}

 {"id":"bd-kpy","title":"Sync race: rebase-based divergence recovery resurrects tombstones","description":"## Problem
When two repos sync simultaneously, tombstones can be resurrected:

1. Repo A deletes issue (creates tombstone), pushes to sync branch
2. Repo B (with 'closed' status) exports and tries to push
3. Push fails (non-fast-forward)
4. fetchAndRebaseInWorktree does git rebase
5. Git rebase applies B's 'closed' patch on top of A's 'tombstone'
6. TEXT-level rebase doesn't invoke beads merge driver
7. 'closed' overwrites 'tombstone' = resurrection

## Root Cause
CommitToSyncBranch uses git rebase for divergence recovery, but rebase is text-level, not content-level. The proper content-level merge in PullFromSyncBranch handles tombstones correctly, but it runs AFTER the problematic push.

## Proposed Fix
Option 1: Don't push in CommitToSyncBranch - let PullFromSyncBranch handle merge+push
Option 2: Replace git rebase with content-level merge in fetchAndRebaseInWorktree
Option 3: Reorder sync steps: Export → Pull/Merge → Commit → Push

## Workaround Applied
Excluded tombstones from orphan detection warnings (commit 1e97d9cc).

See also: bd-3852 (Add orphan detection migration)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-17T23:29:33.049272-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T23:29:33.049272-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-kwro","title":"Beads Messaging & Knowledge Graph (v0.30.2)","description":"Add messaging semantics and extended graph links to Beads, enabling it to serve as
the universal substrate for knowledge work - issues, messages, documents, and threads
as nodes in a queryable graph.

## Motivation

Gas Town (GGT) needs inter-agent communication. Rather than a separate mail system,
collapse messaging into Beads - one system, one sync, one query interface, all in git.

This also positions Beads as a foundation for:
- Company-wide issue tracking (like Notion)
- Threaded conversations (like Reddit/Slack)
- Knowledge graphs with loose associations
- Arbitrary workflow UIs built on top

## New Issue Type

**message** - ephemeral communication between workers
- sender: who sent it
- assignee: recipient
- priority: P0 (urgent) to P4 (routine)
- status: open (unread) -> closed (read)
- ephemeral: true = can be bulk-deleted after swarm

## New Graph Links

**replies_to** - conversation threading
- Messages reply to messages
- Enables Reddit-style nested threads
- Different from parent_id (not hierarchy, its conversation flow)

**relates_to** - loose see also associations
- Bidirectional knowledge graph edges
- Not blocking, not hierarchical, just related
- Enables discovery and traversal

**duplicates** - deduplication at scale
- Mark issue B as duplicate of canonical issue A
- Close B, link to A
- Essential for large issue databases

**supersedes** - version chains
- Design Doc v2 supersedes Design Doc v1
- Track evolution of artifacts

## New Fields (optional, any issue type)

- sender (string) - who created this (for messages)
- ephemeral (boolean) - can be bulk-deleted when closed

## New Commands

Messaging:
- bd mail send <recipient> -s Subject -m Body
- bd mail inbox (list open messages for me)
- bd mail read <id> (show message content)
- bd mail ack <id> (mark as read/close)
- bd mail reply <id> -m Response (reply to thread)

Graph links:
- bd relate <id1> <id2> (create relates_to link)
- bd duplicate <id> --of <canonical> (mark as duplicate)
- bd supersede <id> --with <new> (mark superseded)

Cleanup:
- bd cleanup --ephemeral (delete closed ephemeral issues)

## Identity Configuration

Workers need identity for sender field:
- BEADS_IDENTITY env var
- Or .beads/config.json: identity field

## Hooks (for GGT integration)

Beads as platform - extensible without knowing about GGT.
Hook files in .beads/hooks/:
- on_create (runs after bd create)
- on_update (runs after bd update)
- on_close (runs after bd close)
- on_message (runs after bd mail send)

GGT registers hooks to notify daemons of new messages.

## Schema Changes (Migration Required)

Add to issue schema:
- type: message (new valid type)
- sender: string (optional)
- ephemeral: boolean (optional)
- replies_to: string (issue ID, optional)
- relates_to: []string (issue IDs, optional)
- duplicates: string (canonical issue ID, optional)
- superseded_by: string (new issue ID, optional)

Migration adds fields as optional - existing beads unchanged.

## Success Criteria

1. bd mail send/inbox/read/ack/reply work end-to-end
2. replies_to creates proper thread structure
3. relates_to, duplicates, supersedes links queryable
4. Hooks fire on create/update/close/message
5. Identity configurable via env or config
6. Migration preserves all existing data
7. All new features have tests","status":"tombstone","issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-16T03:00:53.912223-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T16:11:17.070763-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-17T16:11:17.070763-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"epic"}

- internal/models/issue.go - add fields to struct
- internal/storage/sqlite/sqlite.go - CRUD operations

 {"id":"bd-tm2p","title":"Polecats get stuck on interactive shell prompts (cp/mv/rm -i)","description":"During swarm operations, polecats frequently get stuck waiting for interactive prompts from shell commands like:
- cp prompting 'overwrite file? (y/n)'
- mv prompting 'overwrite file? (y/n)'  
- rm prompting 'remove file?'

This happens because macOS aliases or shell configs may have -i flags set by default.

Root cause: Claude Code runs commands that trigger interactive confirmation prompts, but cannot respond to them, causing the agent to hang indefinitely.

Observed in: Multiple polecats during GH issues swarm (Dec 2024)
- Derrick, Roustabout, Prospector, Warboy all got stuck on y/n prompts

Suggested fixes:
1. AGENTS.md should instruct agents to always use -f flag with cp/mv/rm
2. Polecat startup could set shell aliases to use non-interactive versions
3. bd prime hook could include guidance about non-interactive commands
4. Consider detecting stuck prompts and auto-recovering","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-14T16:51:24.572271-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T23:13:40.536312-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T19:13:04.074424-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-y8bj","title":"Auto-detect identity from directory context for bd mail","description":"Currently bd mail inbox defaults to git user name, requiring --identity flag with exact format.

## Problem
- Mail sent to `gastown/crew/max`
- Max runs `bd mail inbox` → defaults to 'Steve Yegge' (git user)
- Max must know to use `--identity 'gastown/crew/max'` with exact slashes

## Proposed Fix
Auto-detect identity from directory context when in a Gas Town workspace:
- In `/Users/stevey/gt/gastown/crew/max`, infer identity = `gastown/crew/max`
- Pattern: `<town>/<rig>/<role>/<name>` → `<rig>/<role>/<name>`

## Additional Improvements
1. Support GT_IDENTITY env var (set by gt crew at / session spawning)
2. Support identity in .beads/config.yaml
3. Normalize format: accept both slashes and dashes as equivalent

## Context
Discovered during crew-to-crew work assignment. Max couldn't see mail despite correct nudge because identity defaulted wrong.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-20T17:22:53.938586-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T18:12:58.472262-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T17:58:51.034201-08:00"}
